money_home_text: Roofing Contractors Tampa | Roof Repair Skip to content Roofing Contractors Tampa Home Services Locations About Tools Contact Enquire Open menu Home Services Locations About Tools Contact Tampa & Hillsborough County Roofing Contractors Tampa A leak after a storm, or a roof that has simply had enough, is stressful - and half the worry is not knowing whether you are being told the truth. We help Tampa homeowners get a straight answer fast, from licensed Florida roofers who quote from a real inspection, not a guess from the driveway. ✓ Repair or replace, based on a real inspection - not a sales pitch ✓ Built to Florida Building Code high-wind standards ✓ Verify any license yourself at MyFloridaLicense.com Get your roof looked at See our roofing services Tell us about your roof Tell us what is happening and a licensed Tampa roofer can inspect the roof and put a price in writing. Your name Phone Email Neighborhood / ZIP code Send My Enquiry Your details are only used to respond to your enquiry. See our privacy policy . Roof leak repair, replacement and roof inspection in Tampa Tampa roofs work hard - heat, UV, afternoon storms, and the wind that comes with hurricane season all shorten their life. Most homeowners land here for one of a few reasons: a fresh leak, storm damage, or an aging roof that needs a plan. Whether the right answer is architectural shingles, barrel tile, standing-seam metal, or a membrane over a low-slope section, we help you weigh it and get to a roofer who can quote the work properly. Roof Repair Fast, reliable repairs for leaks, storm damage and worn roofing across Tampa. View service Roof Replacement Full roof replacement using Florida Building Code-compliant materials and proper ventilation. View service Shingle Roofing Architectural asphalt shingle installation and replacement for Tampa homes. View service Metal Roofing Long-lasting metal roofing systems designed for Florida wind and salt exposure. View service Tile Roofing Concrete and clay tile roofing with code-compliant underlayment for Tampa homes. View service Flat & Low-Slope Roofing TPO and modified bitumen solutions for low-slope roofs and additions. View service Roof Inspection Detailed inspections for storm damage, insurance claims and remaining useful life reports. View service Why homeowners start here A roof is one of the biggest decisions a homeowner makes, and in Florida it carries real code and insurance weight. Here is what we focus on so you can move forward without second-guessing. Verify the license yourself Our network is made up of licensed Florida roofers. You can confirm any license at MyFloridaLicense.com before work begins - no need to take anyone's word for it. Repair or replace, on the evidence The recommendation follows a real inspection of your roof, not a number quoted over the phone. If a repair will do, you hear that first. Built for Florida wind Tampa roofs must meet the Florida Building Code's high-wind requirements, and the roofer builds to those standards on the work they carry out. One clear written estimate You get an itemized estimate after the inspection - material, tear-off, decking, permit, warranty - so you can compare quotes like with like. Straight answers to the worries that keep you up "Do I really need a whole new roof?" Often, no. If the damage is localized and the roof is otherwise sound, a targeted repair is the smarter spend - and a good roofer will tell you so. Replacement earns its cost when leaks keep coming back or the roof has simply aged out. That call should come from someone who has been up on the roof. "How do I know I'm not being ripped off?" Florida roofing is regulated for a reason. You can look up any contractor's license at MyFloridaLicense.com before a dollar changes hands, and a fair estimate is itemized - material, tear-off, decking, permit, warranty - so two quotes can actually be compared. We help you get there. "What about my insurance?" After a storm, documentation matters. A proper inspection records the damage in photos and writing, which is what an insurer needs - and a wind mitigation inspection can even qualify you for a premium discount. The roofer documents; the insurer decides. Roofing help across Tampa From the historic bungalows of Hyde Park and Seminole Heights to the waterfront homes on Davis Islands and the newer builds out in Tampa Palms, roofs across the area face different problems. Pick your neighborhood for local detail. Hyde Park 33606 Ybor City 33605 Seminole Heights 33604 Davis Islands 33606 Westshore 33609 Palma Ceia 33629 Ballast Point 33611 Channel District 33602 Tampa Heights 33602 Carrollwood 33618 Town 'n' Country 33615 Temple Terrace 33617 Sulphur Springs 33604 Tampa Palms 33647 Brandon 33511 Roofing questions Tampa homeowners ask Straight answers on cost, repair versus replacement, permits, and storm damage. How much does a new roof typically cost in Tampa, Florida? Roof replacement costs in Tampa vary based on roof size, pitch, material, and whether code upgrades or decking repairs are required. Homeowners usually get multiple written, itemized estimates from licensed contractors to compare labor, materials, and any permit or inspection-related costs under Florida Building Code. Do I need a permit from the City of Tampa to replace my roof? Yes, residential roof covering replacement in Tampa generally requires a building permit and subsequent inspections to confirm compliance with the Florida Building Code. Most homeowners have their roofing contractor pull the permit, but you should confirm this in writing before work starts. Should I repair my roof or fully replace it after storm damage? Whether you repair or replace depends on the extent of damage, roof age, and whether the roof meets current Florida Building Code standards.
